title: Mildly Smoky Moroccan Vegetable Tagine
description: A fresh and light Moroccan dish featuring smoky olive oil and plant-based ingredients, with a cheese-pull effect from the shio-cure, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the smoky olive oil in a large tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped carrots, zucchini, and bell pepper, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the chickpeas, smoked paprika, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Pour in the dissolved shio-cure and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, until the flavors have melded together and the vegetables are very tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs and a dollop of vegan yogurt, if desired.
Let the tagine cool slightly before serving, allowing the flavors to meld together and the cheese-pull effect to develop.
